member.name: Vikki Slade
member.role: MP for Mid Dorset and North Poole
member.synopsis: Vikki Slade is the Liberal Democrat MP for Mid Dorset and North Poole , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Parking
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will take steps to extend free parking provision for all NHS staff on NHS sites.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Health Service car parking guidance already requires free parking to be provided for four groups: disabled people; frequent outpatient attenders; parents of sick children staying overnight; and staff working night shifts. Beyond this, NHS org...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Free School Meals: Universal Credit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what recent progress she has made on extending free school meals to all children whose families are in receipt of Universal Credit by the September 2026 deadline; and whether eligible families will have to opt-
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to breaking down barriers to opportunity and tackling child poverty. We are extending free school meals (FSM) to all children from households in receipt of Universal Credit from the start of the 2026/27 academic year. We have ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Equipment: Animal Welfare
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether he has made an assessment with Cabinet colleagues of the potential merits of requiring manufacturers of ground maintenance equipment to put stickers or markings on such products to warn users t
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made a specific assessment with Cabinet colleagues of the merits of requiring manufacturers of ground maintenance equipment to place stickers or markings on such products advising users to check for hedgehogs before use.

writtenQuestions.heading: Judicial Conduct Investigations Office
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of legislating for the Judicial Conduct Investigations Office to have the power to investigate sentencing patterns of Judges.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State for Justice has made no assessment of the merits of this proposal.The Judicial Conduct Investigations Office considers complaints about judicial misconduct. It does not review judicial decisions or case management.The proper route f...

writtenQuestions.heading: Taxation: Self-assessment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what recent assessment her Department has made of the a) adequacy of the ease with which people can contact HMRC for support completing self-assessments and b) effectiveness of that support.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HMRC offers a wide range of support to help customers complete their Self Assessment return. This includes support through their helpline, guidance notes and help sheets on GOV.UK, as well as online webinars and recorded videos on YouTube covering various...

writtenQuestions.heading: Neonicotinoids
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department plans to implement a ban on the emergency use of neonicotinoids as a matter of urgency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government remains committed to ending the use in England of the three neonicotinoid pesticides (thiamethoxam, clothianidin, imidacloprid) that threaten vital pollinators, including through emergency authorisations. The Health and Safety Executive (H...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Badgers: Animal Welfare and Disease Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to tackle (a) badger baiting and (b) badger culling in the South West.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Badger persecution is a UK wildlife crime priority, and a police-led Tactical Delivery Group exists to tackle offences against badgers. Defra is providing £530,000 in the financial year 2026-2027 for the National Wildlife Crime Unit, which helps prevent a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Badgers: Animal Welfare and Disease Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what information her Department holds on how many badgers were killed from (a) badger baiting and (b) badger culling in the South West as a proportion of the total number of badgers ki
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra does not hold official figures at national or regional level on the deaths of badgers resulting from badger baiting. The number of badgers culled annually under licence for bovine TB disease control purposes is available at the following link. Numbe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Badgers: Animal Welfare
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she has made an assessment with Cabinet colleagues of the potential merits of raising the maximum prison sentence for badger baiting under the Protection of Badgers Act 1992 to
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government’s Animal Welfare Strategy, published in December 2025, made a commitment to review and look to strengthen penalties for cruelty against wildlife, including the horrific offence of badger baiting, to ensure they are consistent with higher l...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Sector: Occupational Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the change to the SCAPE discount rate on the retirement plans of public sector workers within five years of their expected retirement date.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government announced on 19 May 2026 that the SCAPE discount rate is 2%+CPI. The SCAPE discount rate must be reviewed periodically and calculated in accordance with current methodology. By way of background, the Government published its response to the...

writtenQuestions.heading: Offences against Children: Prisoners' Release
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether offenders servicing sentences for sexual abuse against children are eligible for the Early Release Scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government inherited a prisons system days from collapse. We had no choice but to take decisive action to stop our prisons overflowing and keep the public safe. Our landmark Sentencing Act 2026 will now deliver a more sustainable solution to the pris...

writtenQuestions.heading: Overseas Trade: Occupied Territories
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the potential implications for his policies on trade with illegal Israeli settlements in the Occupied Palestinian Territories of the UK's legal recognition of the State o
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK’s trade relations with the Palestinian Authority are governed by the UK-PA Interim Political, Trade and Partnership Agreement, which applies to the territory of the West Bank and Gaza. The UK recognition of Palestine is based on provisional 1967 bo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cricket: South West
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, further to the Written Statement of 9 June 2026 on Grassroots Cricket, if she will take steps to invest in multi-sport covered cricket domes in the South West of England, including Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is determined to ensure that everyone has access to quality sport and physical activity opportunities. That's why we are investing £400 million into new and upgraded grassroots sport facilities, promoting health, wellbeing and community coh...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Overseas Trade: Israeli Settlements
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the potential implications for his policies of the International Court of Justice’s advisory opinion of 19 July 2024 on Israel’s occupation of the Palestinian territories
writtenQuestions.answerText: The ICJ’s Advisory Opinion of July 2024 contains important findings, many of which are already government policy and align with the UK’s views that the Israeli occupation of Palestine is illegal.The UK government does not recognise illegal Israeli settlem...

writtenQuestions.heading: Coeliac Disease: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of supporting a pharmacy-led gluten free service in England, with annual health checks and collaboration between pharmacies, GPs and dieticians
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Health and Social Care has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Forests: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she plans to negotiate for alignment of deforestation regulations between the UK and the EU as part of the Sanitary and Phytosanitary Agreement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is strengthening its approach to tackling deforestation in UK supply chains. In Great Britain, Defra will consult on the department’s aim to introduce an approach which works with the EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR), including under the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Israel: Palestine
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, when she will set out a timeline for the publication of a formal response to the International Court of Justice’s advisory opinion of 19 July 2024 on the legal consequences o
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK's position remains as set out in the response to Question 83315 on 29 October 2025. We will update the House as and when there are further developments to report.

writtenQuestions.heading: Israeli Settlements

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Minister for Creative Industries, Media and Arts and I received a response from the Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) on 19 June. The ASA confirmed it is considering a complaint and assessing whether the material falls within the scope of the Adve...

writtenQuestions.heading: Russia: Subversion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if his Department will launch an inquiry into the level of threat of potential interference from the Russian Government in UK politics to help inform his Department's policies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The MOD, alongside other government departments, keeps state threats, including interference efforts, from Russia under constant review.It is an absolute priority for this government to protect our politics and democratic processes from foreign interferen...

writtenQuestions.heading: Iron and Steel: Quotas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of proposed changes to quotas for Category 26 of the UK's Steel Safeguard Measures on downstream manufacturers reliant on imported stainless steel tu
writtenQuestions.answerText: This is not a continuation of the steel safeguard, but a new steel trade measure to address the serious threat posed by global steel overcapacity.The Government has engaged extensively with industry, including downstream users, to assess impacts. The meas...
